_comment: Bidirectional MyChart round-trip: provider Result Note in (6:51 AM) → outbound MyChart message (10:07 AM) → patient inbound reply (10:15 AM) — bucket transition from results_followup to pt_advice_request mid-investigation. Time tokens resolved at load time to keep the demo fresh.
title: Statin follow-up: LDL response + AST elevation
clinicalConcern: LDL 95 (target met). AST 42 (mildly elevated, ULN 35) on new atorvastatin 40 mg started ~6 weeks ago — likely med-related, monitor.

touchpoints.summary: Dr. Ballinger Result Note: 5-question follow-up template

data.subject: Statin follow-up — LDL/LFT recheck
data.body:
Pt with new statin 6 weeks ago. LDL 95 (target met), AST 42 (mildly up, ULN 35). Please follow up:

1. Any muscle aches/cramps/weakness?
2. Tea-colored urine or unusual fatigue?
3. New supplements, OTC meds, or dietary changes since starting?
4. Tolerating once-daily evening dose?
5. Reinforce continue current dose, recheck LFTs in 6 weeks.

Draft MyChart message and Nurse Note for my cosign.

data.subject: Your recent lab results — quick check-in
data.body:
Hi! Your recent lab results are back from a couple of weeks ago and Dr. Ballinger wanted me to follow up with you about a few quick things now that you've been on the new cholesterol pill (atorvastatin) for about six weeks.

Great news first: your bad cholesterol number (LDL) came down nicely to 95, which is in the goal range — the medicine is doing its job.

One of the liver enzymes we check (called AST) was a tiny bit above the normal range (42, normal goes up to 35). This is a fairly common thing we see when someone starts a new cholesterol medicine, and it's usually not a problem. We'd just like to keep an eye on it.

A few quick questions for you, please reply when you have a moment:

1. Any new muscle aches, cramps, or weakness anywhere — especially in your legs or back?
2. Have you noticed your urine looking darker than usual (tea-colored), or felt unusually tired?
3. Have you started any new vitamins, supplements, or over-the-counter medicines since you started the cholesterol pill?
4. Are you taking the pill once a day in the evening, and is that working for you?

For now, please keep taking the medicine the same way. We'll plan to recheck a small blood test in about 6 weeks just to make sure the liver number trends back down. No need to come in.

Thanks!
Brandon, RN — Riverbend Cardiology

touchpoints.summary: Inbound MyChart: patient reply (introduces turmeric supplement)

data.subject: Re: Your recent lab results — quick check-in
data.body: Thanks for checking in! No muscle aches at all, urine looks normal, energy is fine. I started a turmeric supplement about 3 weeks ago for my joints — is that okay with the cholesterol med? I take it once at night. Happy to keep going on the cholesterol med.
